curation.synopsis: Horus Aximand — Little Horus, fifth captain of the Sons of Horus and youngest of the Mournival — has been dreaming. A figure in his sleep walks beside him with a face he cannot quite see, and he wakes uneasy at every dawn of his Legion's new war. Tasked with subjugating Dwell, an Imperial world whose great mausoleum-engines preserve the memories of the honoured dead, Aximand plans the assault with Grael Noctua, then leads his men down into a battle that should be easy. It is not. The Iron Hands captain Shadrak Meduson has brought a Shattered-Legions kill-team to Dwell, including a White Scars hunter named Hibou Khan. By the time the mausoleum belongs to the Sons of Horus, Aximand has been bested in single combat, a brother has died saving him, and Khan's blade has taken away the face he was named for. The dream walks closer now, and the face within it is finally clear.
